devdoc.details: This implements an optional extension of {ERC721} defined in the EIP that adds enumerability of all the token ids in the contract as well as all token ids owned by each account.

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "ERC721Enumerable: owner index out of bounds"

arguments.value: ERC721Enumerable: owner index out of bounds

argumentTypes.typeString: literal_string "ERC721Enumerable: owner index out of bounds"

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "ERC721Enumerable: global index out of bounds"

arguments.value: ERC721Enumerable: global index out of bounds

argumentTypes.typeString: literal_string "ERC721Enumerable: global index out of bounds"

documentation.text:
@dev Hook that is called before any token transfer. This includes minting
 and burning.
 Calling conditions:
 - When `from` and `to` are both non-zero, ``from``'s `tokenId` will be
 transferred to `to`.
 - When `from` is zero, `tokenId` will be minted for `to`.
 - When `to` is zero, ``from``'s `tokenId` will be burned.
 - `from` cannot be the zero address.
 - `to` cannot be the zero address.
 To learn more about hooks, head to xref:ROOT:extending-contracts.adoc#using-hooks[Using Hooks].

@dev Private function to add a token to this extension's token tracking data structures.
 @param tokenId uint256 ID of the token to be added to the tokens list
